title: Palestinian refugees forced to flee the southern Lebanon: 'All my life, I have only known displacement'
subhead: Evacuation orders from the Israeli army, which signal incoming bombardments, now include for the first time all three Palestinian camps in the Tyre region. Issued on Wednesday and renewed the following day, these orders forced thousands of Palestinian refugees to flee their homes.

title: French sociologist, philosopher and intellectual provocateur Edgar Morin has died aged 104

Patrick Artus: 'Wars are economically irrational. Yet, the risk of new conflicts remains high'
subhead: The economist outlines five main reasons why the militarization of international relations is on the rise.

title: French Open 2026: Moïse Kouamé's meteoric rise
subhead: Poised for greatness, the 17-year-old French player has won his first two matches at the Paris Grand Slam and faces Chilean Alejandro Tabilo on Saturday in the third round. It is a spectacular ascent for a player already recognized as a young phenomenon.

title: The little-known story of Bella Ariel, a Lanvin model murdered at Auschwitz
subhead: Journalist and author Arnaud Nemet tells the story of his great-grand-aunt, Bella Ariel, a celebrated 1930s model whose life was both dazzling and tragic. A Jewish immigrant from Constantinople, she was arrested after a denunciation and murdered at Auschwitz-Birkenau at 31.

title: José Tolentino de Mendonça, the poet cardinal reconciling the Church and contemporary art
subhead: The Portuguese prelate has become a prominent figure in the world of contemporary art, befriending artists and film stars. As the driving force behind the Vatican Pavilion at the recently opened Venice Biennale, he has used his progressive outlook to help bridge the Church and a world that long viewed it with suspicion.

The Bolloré protégée spreading Russian propaganda in France
subhead: Xenia Fedorova, the former director of the now-banned Russian channel RT France, has free rein to advance the Kremlin's version of events without contradiction across the media empire of far-right billionaire Vincent Bolloré.

title: The resilience of French growth appears to be reaching its limits
subhead: France's gross domestic product for the first quarter of 2026 has been revised down to -0.1%. While the government blames 'wait-and-see' behavior, several indicators suggest a worsening economic climate.

title: Iran is gradually reconnecting to the internet, though access remains limited
subhead: After a three-month blackout, Iran regained partial internet access on Tuesday, May 26. The Intelligence Ministry has warned Iranians against collaborating with any foreign media, which it accused of espionage.
